grammar: no object, with adverbial of direction
with object and adverbial of direction
with object and usually with adverbial of direction

senses.definition: move or cause to move in a specified direction
examples: : he passed through towns and villages
: he passed a weary hand across his forehead
pass an electric current through it
the shells from the Allied guns were passing very low overhead
registers: no object, with adverbial of direction
with object and adverbial of direction

senses.definition: change from one state or condition to another
examples: homes that have passed from public to private ownership

senses.definition: die (used euphemistically)
examples: his father had passed to the afterlife

senses.definition: go past or across; leave behind or on one side in proceeding
examples: the two vehicles had no room to pass each other
: we will not let you pass
she passed a rest area with a pay phone

senses.definition: go beyond the limits of; surpass or exceed
examples: this item has passed its sell-by date

senses.definition: hit a winning shot past (an opponent).
elapse; go by
examples: the day and night passed slowly
the moment had passed
senses.semanticHint: (of time or a point in time)

senses.definition: spend or use up (a period of time)
examples: this was how they passed the time

senses.definition: come to an end
examples: the danger had passed

senses.definition: happen; be done or said
examples: not another word passed between them
: this fact has passed almost unnoticed

senses.definition: transfer (something) to someone, especially by handing or bequeathing it to the next person in a series
examples: your letter has been passed to Mr. Rich for action
: he passed her a cup
please pass the fish
registers: with object and usually with adverbial of direction

senses.definition: be transferred from one person or place to another, especially by inheritance
examples: if Ann remarried the estate would pass to her new husband
infections can pass from mother to child at birth

senses.definition: throw, kick, or hit (the ball or puck) to another player on one's own team
examples: his intent was to pass the ball forward rather than knock it back
senses.semanticHint: (in football, soccer, hockey, and other games)

senses.definition: put (something, especially money) into circulation
examples: persons who have passed bad checks

senses.definition: circulate; be current
examples: cash was passing briskly

senses.definition: be successful in (an examination, test, or course)
examples: she passed her driving test

senses.definition: judge the performance or standard of (someone or something) to be satisfactory
examples: : he was passed fit by army doctors

senses.definition: be accepted as adequate; go uncensured
examples: she couldn't agree, but let it pass
her dress passed without comment

senses.definition: be accepted or perceived as belonging to an ethnic group other than one's own or as having a sex other than the one registered at birth
examples: when I went through my transition I no longer tried to pass
how dark one could be and still pass varied from region to region

senses.definition: approve or put into effect (a proposal or law) by voting on it
examples: the bill was passed despite fierce opposition
senses.semanticHint: (of a legislative or other official body)

senses.definition: be examined and approved by (a legislative body or process)
examples: the Bill passed by 164 votes to 107

senses.definition: pronounce (a judgment or judicial sentence)
examples: passing judgment on these crucial issues
it is now my duty to pass sentence upon you

senses.definition: utter (something, especially criticism)
examples: she would pass remarks about the Paxtons in their own house

senses.definition: adjudicate or give a judgment on a matter
examples: a jury could not be trusted to pass upon the question of Endicott's good faith

senses.definition: discharge (something, especially urine or feces) from the body
examples: frequency of passing urine

senses.definition: forgo one's turn in a game or an offered opportunity
examples: we pass on dessert and have coffee

senses.definition: not declare or pay (a dividend)
examples: the company has already passed its interim dividend

senses.definition: make no bid when it is one's turn during an auction
examples: South bids 1NT. North passes

senses.definition: an act or instance of moving past or through something
examples: repeated passes with the swipe card
an unmarked plane had been making passes over his house

senses.definition: an act of passing the hands over anything, as in conjuring or hypnotism.
a thrust in fencing.
a juggling trick.
an act of refraining from bidding during the auction.
a single scan through a set of data or a program.
a successful completion of an examination or course
examples: : a 100 percent pass rate

senses.definition: the grade indicating the successful completion of an examination or course.
an achievement of a university degree without honors
examples: : a pass degree

senses.definition: a card, ticket, or permit giving authorization for the holder to enter or have access to a place, form of transportation, or event
examples: a bus pass
you could only get in with a pass

senses.definition: toleration of bad behavior or poor performance
examples: businesses should not get a pass to be reckless
that doesn't give him a pass to say things without reproach

senses.definition: an identity book that Black people had to carry between 1952 and 1986, used to limit the movement of Black people to urban areas.
an act of throwing, kicking, or hitting the ball or puck to another player on the same team
examples: his cross-field pass to Giggs
senses.semanticHint: (in football, soccer, hockey, and other games)

senses.definition: an amorous or sexual advance made to someone
examples: she made a pass at Stephen

senses.definition: a rejection or dismissal
examples: those who don't like oily food may want to give this a pass
I took a pass on his request for help

senses.definition: a state or situation of a specified, usually bad or difficult, nature
examples: things came to such a pass that these gentlemen sat coldly at the meetings not daring to speak out freely and honestly
this is a sad pass for a fixture that used to crackle with excitement

senses.definition: an act of refraining from bidding during the auction.

exclamation said when one does not know the answer to a question, for example in a quizzing game: to the enigmatic question we answered “Pass.”. • US English informal used to express rejection or dismissal: the shepherd's pie looks gross. Pass!.

notes.content: Middle English: from Old French passer, based on Latin passus ‘pace’.

items.definition: reach a bad or regrettable state of affairs
examples: if this was what was being taught these days in colleges things had come to a pretty pass
she passed away in her sleep

items.definition: happen without being noticed or fully experienced by someone
examples: sometimes I feel that life is passing me by

items.definition: falsely represent a person or thing as being someone or something else
examples: the drink was packaged in champagne bottles and was being passed off as the real stuff
he made a light joke and passed it off
the weekend had passed off entirely without incident
he scored eight times and passed off six assists
his wife passed on twelve years ago
he consumed enough alcohol to make him pass out

items.definition: ignore the claims of someone to promotion or advancement
examples: he was passed over for a cabinet job
I shall pass over the matter of the transitional period
by the time I reached the hospital he had passed over

items.definition: refrain from taking up an opportunity
examples: he passed up a career in pro baseball
